title: Fix Obsidian plugin artifact loading for real runtime

do_not_read: other directories in ~/dev unless explicitly referenced

scope: Make clients/obsidian usable as a real Obsidian plugin artifact in a disposable vault. Address the missing root main.js and module-resolution issue found by MVP-071. Keep provider logic out of the client.
goals: Use the tdd skill discipline: reproduce/encode the packaging/load expectation, implement the smallest artifact/build fix, then rerun tests.
Provide a root-level Obsidian plugin artifact or build script that gives Obsidian root manifest.json, main.js, and styles.css.
Ensure Obsidian runtime imports for obsidian and CodeMirror resolve in the actual plugin host, while local Node tests can still cover pure logic.
Update docs/client-setup.md and clients/README.md so the documented disposable-vault setup matches the real artifact.
Add or update tests/static checks to prevent regression to a missing root main.js artifact.
non_goals: Do not implement provider logic in the Obsidian client.
Do not change broker protocol or provider behavior.
Do not run real pi unless needed by a verifier; this task should focus on artifact/loadability.
safety: Use disposable temp paths for any local packaging checks.
success_criteria: node --check or an equivalent syntax/build check passes for the produced Obsidian main artifact.
A disposable-vault artifact layout check confirms Obsidian sees root manifest.json/main.js/styles.css.
